title: Wardrobes & Custom Storage
heroAlt: Custom built-in wardrobe with full-height cabinetry and integrated storage — InsideOut Joinery Sydney
metaTitle: Custom Wardrobes Sydney | Built-In & Walk-In | InsideOut Joinery & Renos
metaDescription: Custom built-in and walk-in wardrobes across Sydney — designed around your room and what you actually need to store. Free quotes. Call 02 5000 0402.
intro: The problem with most wardrobes is not the wardrobe — it is that nobody thought about what was going in it. A flat-pack unit gives you fixed shelf heights and a standard hanging rail. A wardrobe built specifically for your room, your clothes and the way you get dressed in the morning is a completely different thing. We design and build built-in wardrobes, walk-in robes and custom storage across Sydney — starting with what you actually need to store, not with a showroom configuration.
includedTitle: What a wardrobe or storage project can include
included: Built-in wardrobes designed to fill the full height and width of the wall — no gaps at the top, no filler strips at the side
Walk-in robe fitouts planned around hanging length, folded clothing, shoes and accessories — not just shelves and a rail
Sliding door systems where swing clearance is tight or the room calls for it
Bedroom storage walls that combine robe, display shelving and bedside cabinetry into one clean run
Internal configurations including double hanging, long hanging, shoe racks, drawer banks, pull-out tie storage and jewellery trays
Joinery integrated with existing cornicing and skirting so it reads as part of the room, not placed into it

featureBlocks.title: Start with what goes in it
featureBlocks.text: Before we talk about door profiles or handle styles, we ask what you actually need to store. How many hanging items? How much folded clothing? Shoes, bags, suits, kids' clothes? The answers shape the internal layout — and the internal layout is what makes a wardrobe either genuinely useful or just a better-looking version of what you had before.

featureBlocks.title: Built to the room, not to a module
featureBlocks.text: A wardrobe that fills the full wall height and sits flush with the ceiling looks like it was always part of the house. A wardrobe built to a standard module height with a gap at the top looks like it was purchased and placed. The difference is visible immediately and affects how the room feels. We build to the actual dimensions of your room — ceiling height, cornicing, sloped ceilings, awkward walls and all.

featureBlocks.title: Walk-in robes that work as a room
featureBlocks.text: The walk-in robes that feel genuinely good to use are the ones where the lighting is right, the hanging heights are set for the person using them, and there is a logical flow between sections. We plan walk-ins with the same level of detail we apply to a kitchen layout — because the same logic applies. Get the brief right, and the space works every single day.

materials: Two-pac polyurethane doors in matte or satin — the cleanest, most durable painted finish for bedroom joinery
Timber veneer and timber-look laminate for warmth in rooms where white cabinetry would feel cold
Shaker door profiles for homes where a more classic or period-sympathetic look is right
Frameless and profile-framed sliding door systems in a range of configurations and glass insert options
Soft-close hinges and drawer runners as standard — not an upgrade
Integrated LED strip lighting inside robes and under overhead cabinetry so the space is actually usable at night
Brushed nickel, matte black and chrome handles to suit the room's existing hardware direction

beforeAfter.title: Before & after
beforeAfter.text: The Beaumont Hills project shows what happens when bedroom storage is treated as a design problem rather than a box-ticking exercise. Same room — completely different experience.
beforeAfter.beforeAlt: Beaumont Hills bedroom before — existing storage
beforeAfter.afterAlt: Beaumont Hills bedroom after — custom built-in wardrobe and integrated storage wall

faqs.question: What is the difference between a built-in wardrobe and a walk-in robe?
faqs.answer: A built-in wardrobe sits within the bedroom — doors close across the front, and you access it from outside the cabinetry. A walk-in robe is a dedicated space you step into, typically open-fronted, where everything is visible and accessible at once. Both are built to your exact dimensions and storage requirements.

faqs.question: How much does a custom built-in wardrobe cost?
faqs.answer: A single-bay built-in wardrobe starts from around $3,000 to $5,000 depending on size, door style and internal fitout. Walk-in robes vary more depending on the room size and how detailed the internal layout needs to be. We quote each job specifically — there is no useful ballpark without knowing what the space is.

faqs.question: Can you build a wardrobe in a room with a sloped ceiling or unusual shape?
faqs.answer: That is exactly the kind of problem custom joinery is built for. Flat-pack wardrobes cannot deal with sloped ceilings, low beams or odd wall angles. We measure the actual space and build around it.

faqs.question: Do you do the installation as well as the build?
faqs.answer: Yes. We design, build and install everything. One team from start to finish.

faqs.question: Can you match a new wardrobe to existing joinery already in the house?
faqs.answer: Yes. If there is joinery elsewhere in the home we are working around — a kitchen, a bathroom vanity, built-in shelving — we can match door profiles, finishes and hardware so the new wardrobe feels consistent rather than added later.

ctaTitle: Want a wardrobe that actually works?
ctaText: Tell us the room dimensions, what you need to store and what is not working about the current setup. We will come back with a layout and a quote.
